key_insight: CORAL uses reinforcement learning to teach a docking model the cooperative stacking behavior unique to amyloid fibrils, improving ligand pose prediction beyond standard protein docking methods.
engineering_relevance: The method can accelerate design of amyloid-targeting probes and therapeutics by better predicting how small molecules bind to fibril grooves.
method: Generative docking model trained with reinforcement learning
Reward combines protein-ligand affinity and ligand-ligand cooperative stacking energy
Designed for cross-β groove binding geometry in amyloid fibrils
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on experimentally resolved amyloid-ligand structures and a curated expert-validated set, showing better pose quality and affinity correlation than baseline dockers.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and breadth of scarce amyloid-ligand structural data, so generalization to new fibril targets may still be limited.
paper_title: CORAL: Learning Amyloid Fibril Ligand Docking with Cooperative Binding Rewards
